description_long: This fence consists of four vertical posts made of a material resembling wood, supporting two horizontal rails that appear to be painted or coated in white. The rails are staggered, with each segment offset from the next, creating a visually interesting, non-continuous line. The posts are rectangular in cross-section and have flat tops, with no decorative caps or finials. The overall design is simple and utilitarian, with no ornamental features or embellishments. The construction suggests it is intended as a low barrier, suitable for demarcating boundaries in urban parks, sidewalks, or residential areas. The finish of the rails and posts appears matte, and the structure is likely lightweight and movable.
description: A simple, low fence with four brown posts and two staggered white horizontal rails, featuring a flat top and no decorative elements. The design is utilitarian and suitable for urban or residential boundaries.
description_view_0: Front view showing four brown posts and two staggered white rails, with the offset pattern clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view showing the thin profile of the fence, with the rails and posts aligned in depth.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the same four posts and two rails, but the staggered pattern is less pronounced.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin profile and alignment of the rails and posts.
